evaluation_inputs.system_description: WIN-T is the Army's tactical telecommunications system designed to provide a secure, high-speed, high-capacity, and high-mobility backbone communications network. It enables mobile communications using the Internet Protocol (IP) to provide integrated voice, data, and video. It connects the Global Information Grid (GIG) to the Future Force, extending from the Maneuver Battalion to the Theater Rear boundary.

phase_1_discovery.initial_documentation_status: The documentation was recently consolidated following the merger of two competing contractor teams. As a result, the initial architecture documentation contained inconsistencies, was excessively complex, and diagrams often had mixed heritage, leading to confusion and potential errors.
evaluator_inquiry_questions: How will the system handle the self-organization of nodes when requirements for the algorithms are not fully defined?
What are the specific interface definitions for the Service-Oriented Architecture given the reliance on emerging standards?
How does the architecture accommodate the transition from radio firmware to high-level network operations without explicit performance models?

hardware_view_description: The system must support mobile nodes (vehicles) communicating via Line-of-Sight radios and satellite links.
The topology includes tiered nodes ranging from Maneuver Battalions to Theater Rear boundaries, requiring 'on-the-move' connectivity.
runtime_constraints: Network bandwidth is severely limited during peak operational traffic, requiring strict QoS throttling.
The system must support 'on-the-move' throughput while traveling at speeds up to 25-45 mph.

questions: How does the Enterprise Application Integration Platform handle message latency across low-bandwidth satellite links?
How is loose coupling maintained between the NetOps management software and the radio transmission subsystems?

raw_candidate_scenarios.description: A collection of vehicles collects in a 'parking lot' during operations. The networks can self-organize and nodes configure themselves within 5 minutes.
A COTS product is becoming increasingly incompatible with other COTS products being used. The COTS product is replaced.
A unit is overrun during operations. The system detects the unauthorized users and generates a security alert; an authorized person can 'zeroize' the system and shut it down remotely.
A WIN-T gateway node has been corrupted due to a network intrusion. Intrusion and corruption is detected and another node takes over duties.

business_goals: Provide a single integrating communications network to function as the Army's tactical portion of the GIG
Link FCS with higher Army echelons and the GIG
Provide a reliable and secure network with high speed, high capacity, and high quality of service (QoS)
Support mobile communications and replace legacy systems (MSE, TRI-TAC, ISYSCON, Trojan Spirit)
Deploy a user-friendly human machine interface (HMI) for the WIN-T operator
Minimize the amount of network management the WIN-T operator must perform (automate planning)
Operable and maintainable within budget and schedule
Transparent to combat users and operators
technical_constraints: Long life cycle (fielding from 2008 through 2020, expected lifetime 20+ years)
Interoperable with Army Enterprise network, FCS, Joint networks, and Coalition network
Upgradeable (early spirals, block upgrades, COTS upgrades, security upgrades)

architectural_approaches.description: The system is structured around loosely-coupled, interoperable services (e.g., communication, security, management services) that communicate via standard protocols. This enables integration of diverse voice, data, and video capabilities across the tactical network.

addressed_attributes.rationale: Standardized service contracts and protocols allow different system components and external systems (e.g., GIG) to communicate and exchange data seamlessly.
Functionality is encapsulated into discrete services, allowing for independent development, deployment, and replacement.
New capabilities can be added as new services, and existing services can be recomposed to meet evolving mission needs without major system overhaul.

architectural_approaches.description: A central communication backbone (likely an Enterprise Service Bus - ESB pattern) facilitates reliable, asynchronous message exchange between distributed services and components across the mobile network.

addressed_attributes.rationale: The bus provides decoupled communication, message queuing, and guaranteed delivery mechanisms, enhancing reliability in unstable tactical network conditions.
Supports publish-subscribe patterns, enabling efficient one-to-many distribution of critical situational data and orders.
Decoupling via the bus allows components to fail independently and facilitates rerouting of messages, improving overall system survivability.

children.refinement: System components and external systems (Army Enterprise, FCS, Joint, Coalition) exchange data seamlessly via standardized service contracts and protocols.

scenarios.scenario_text: A collection of vehicles collects in a 'parking lot' during operations. The networks can self-organize and nodes configure themselves within 5 minutes.
A COTS product is becoming increasingly incompatible with other COTS products being used. The COTS product is replaced.
A unit is overrun during operations. The system detects the unauthorized users and generates a security alert; an authorized person can 'zeroize' the system and shut it down remotely.
A WIN-T gateway node has been corrupted due to a network intrusion. Intrusion and corruption is detected and another node takes over duties.

scenario_reference.text: A collection of vehicles collects in a 'parking lot' during operations. The networks can self-organize and nodes configure themselves within 5 minutes.

analysis_records.environment: Dynamic, mobile tactical network during force assembly.
analysis_records.stimulus: Multiple mobile nodes (vehicles) converge into a dense, ad-hoc network.
analysis_records.response_measure: Network self-organizes and nodes self-configure within 5 minutes.
architectural_decisions: Use of dynamic, self-organizing network protocols.
Implementation of a decoupled Message Bus for service communication.
analysis_records.reasoning: The Message Bus supports decoupled, asynchronous communication which is resilient to changing network topology. However, the 5-minute target is a strict performance requirement for the self-organizing protocols. The bus adds overhead; if protocol messages are large or frequent, latency during convergence could exceed the target.

risks.description: Self-organization protocols may not meet the 5-minute convergence target under high node density or poor RF conditions.
risks.rationale: The analysis lacks quantitative data on protocol performance, node count, and bandwidth, making this target unvalidated.

risks.description: The Message Bus could become a single point of failure or bottleneck during rapid network formation.
risks.rationale: If the bus instance is centralized or has limited throughput, the flood of discovery and configuration messages during convergence could degrade performance.

non_risks.description: The decoupled nature of the Message Bus inherently supports dynamic topology changes.
non_risks.rationale: Services communicate via the bus without direct dependencies, allowing nodes to join/leave without breaking point-to-point connections.

sensitivity_points.parameter: Message Bus throughput and latency during peak load

scenario_reference.text: A COTS product is becoming increasingly incompatible with other COTS products being used. The COTS product is replaced.

analysis_records.environment: Long-term system evolution over a 20+ year lifecycle.
analysis_records.stimulus: A COTS product requires replacement due to incompatibility.
analysis_records.response_measure: The product is replaced with minimal system disruption and reintegration effort.
architectural_decisions: Adoption of Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA) with standardized service contracts.
Encapsulation of COTS functionality within discrete, replaceable services.
analysis_records.reasoning: SOA promotes loose coupling and well-defined interfaces. If the COTS product is encapsulated behind a stable service contract, it can be swapped. Success depends on the contract's quality and the degree to which the COTS product's proprietary features are abstracted.

risks.description: Service contracts may be poorly designed or become outdated, creating tight coupling to the specific COTS product.
risks.rationale: If the contract mirrors the COTS API, replacing it requires changes to all service consumers, negating the benefit.

non_risks.description: The SOA style fundamentally supports component replacement.
non_risks.rationale: Modularity and standardized interfaces are core tenets of SOA, directly addressing this scenario.

sensitivity_points.parameter: Stability and completeness of service contracts

tradeoff_points.parameter: Level of abstraction in service contracts
conflicting_attributes: Performance (negative: potential for extra abstraction layers)

scenario_reference.text: A unit is overrun during operations. The system detects the unauthorized users and generates a security alert; an authorized person can 'zeroize' the system and shut it down remotely.

analysis_records.stimulus: Unauthorized physical access to a node.
analysis_records.response_measure: 1. Detect unauthorized access. 2. Generate alert. 3. Enable remote secure shutdown (zeroize).
architectural_decisions: Implementation of intrusion detection and alerting services.
Secure, authenticated remote command channel (likely via the Message Bus).
analysis_records.reasoning: Detection relies on monitoring services. The remote zeroize command must be authenticated, integrity-protected, and delivered reliably even if the node is partially compromised. The Message Bus can facilitate secure command dissemination but must itself be secured.

risks.description: Compromised node may ignore or block remote zeroize commands, or falsify detection alerts.
risks.rationale: If an attacker gains sufficient control, they can subvert the local security agent and communication stack.

risks.description: Lack of quantitative targets for detection time and command execution time.
risks.rationale: Without specific timing requirements, the effectiveness of the security response is undefined.

non_risks.description: A dedicated security service architecture aligns with the need for centralized monitoring and response.
non_risks.rationale: SOA allows for specialized security services to be developed and integrated.

sensitivity_points.parameter: Latency and reliability of the secure command channel under attack

scenario_reference.text: A WIN-T gateway node has been corrupted due to a network intrusion. Intrusion and corruption is detected and another node takes over duties.

analysis_records.stimulus: A critical gateway node is corrupted.
analysis_records.response_measure: 1. Detect intrusion and corruption. 2. Automatically failover duties to a healthy node.
architectural_decisions: Health monitoring and intrusion detection services.
Redundant node configuration and failover mechanisms.
Message Bus to facilitate rerouting of traffic.
analysis_records.reasoning: Detection is similar to RS-18. Failover requires: a) pre-designated or dynamically elected backup nodes, b) state replication or session recovery mechanisms for gateway duties, and c) rapid re-routing of network traffic. The Message Bus aids in disseminating failover commands and rerouting messages.

risks.description: Failover may cause service interruption or data loss if gateway state is not adequately replicated.
risks.rationale: Real-time state synchronization is complex and may impact performance. Lack of data on acceptable downtime.

risks.description: The corruption (e.g., malware) might propagate to backup nodes during state synchronization.
risks.rationale: Without clean-slate failover mechanisms, corrupted data or code could be transferred.

non_risks.description: The decoupled communication via the Message Bus simplifies rerouting traffic away from a failed node.
non_risks.rationale: Message endpoints are service-based, not node-based, allowing the bus to redirect to a new provider.

sensitivity_points.parameter: Frequency and granularity of node health state replication
Time to detect corruption and trigger failover

conflicting_attributes: Performance/Network Load (negative: consumes bandwidth and node resources)
